lesson: Critical payment defects should block release until transaction success rate is above 99%.
Critical security findings must not be accepted without compensating controls.
Unstable or failed builds should trigger automatic NO-GO recommendation.
Regression pass rate above 97% correlates strongly with stable rollout.
Closed critical defects must be verified with retest evidence before approval.
Checkout APIs exceeding 3 second SLA create customer abandonment risk.
Test automation evidence reduces CAB review time.
More than two open critical defects historically resulted in rollback or release deferral.
Production monitoring dashboard should be validated before release approval.
Early business sign-off improves release confidence.
